title: Cosplay Faux Pas
body:
Hello and Welcome to Drunk-Robot.com.

Drunk-Robot.com is a webcomic released every Monday. It is written by me, Brian Powell, and drawn by Jeff Pina. If you like this be sure to check out more of Jeff’s stuff at Sideways8studios.com. Also stop by the Sideways 8 Studios booth #547 at the Phoenix Comicon, May 26th-29th!

Thank you, and hope you enjoy!
transcript:
Panel 1. Caption: How girls handle cosplay faux pas. At a convention, two women in identical Slave Leia costumes glare at each other. One says: "I told you to call me so this wouldn't happen!"
Panel 2. Caption: How guys handle it. Two men in identical Boba Fett armor point at each other, thumbs up, and shout: "DUUUUUDE!!!"
altText: Two-panel comic at a convention. Two women in identical Slave Leia costumes glare at each other; one says she told the other to call so this wouldn't happen. In the second panel, two men in identical Boba Fett armor point at each other and shout DUUUUUDE!!!
hoverText: Twin Leias start a blood feud. Twin Fetts activate instant-bro protocol.

title: These Still Aren’t the Droids You’re Looking For
transcript: Single panel. On Tatooine, outside a domed hut, the Tenth Doctor pops out of a golden Dalek casing waving his sonic screwdriver at a stormtrooper, with a Cyberman standing beside him and the Millennium Falcon flying overhead. The Doctor says: "I don't CARE what the last bloke told you, these AREN'T the droids you're looking for!" The stormtrooper looks away, unconvinced.
altText: The Tenth Doctor from Doctor Who leans out of a gold Dalek shell on Tatooine, waving his sonic screwdriver at a skeptical stormtrooper. A Cyberman stands nearby and the Millennium Falcon flies overhead.
hoverText: Obi-Wan waves a hand. The Doctor waves a screwdriver. The stormtrooper just wants his shift to end.

title: Total Reboot
transcript:
Panel 1. In a studio office, an executive tells his boss: "Sir, I've figured out the best way to guarantee that people will see a movie over and over again!" The boss, facepalming: "How?"
Panel 2. The executive grins and raises a finger: "Rekall!"
Panel 3. The TOTAL RECALL title logo.
Panel 4. Outside a theater, a man says: "I've... I've SEEN this movie before... haven't I?" A woman answers: "Let's check it out..."
altText: Four-panel comic. A grinning studio executive pitches Rekall as the way to make audiences watch a movie over and over. Under the Total Recall logo, a couple outside a theater can't remember whether they've already seen it and go in anyway.
hoverText: Get your ass to the box office. Again.

title: Space, Time & Improbability
transcript:
Panel 1. A gleaming spherical starship hangs above Earth and announces: "Improbability Drive Initiating."
Panel 2. A pot of daisies floats alone in space above Earth.
Panel 3. The TARDIS drifts through the star field.
Panel 4. A blue whale tumbles through space and cries: "Wait, wait! Go back!"
altText: Four space panels above Earth. A spherical starship announces its Improbability Drive is initiating; a pot of flowers floats in space; the TARDIS drifts by; a blue whale tumbling through the void yells wait, wait, go back.

title: Katy Perry
transcript:
Panel 1. A gray girl robot with orange hair, a bow, and glasses says: "So, I just heard the new Katy Perry song."
Panel 2. She continues: "I'm now convinced she's making music for strip clubs and just marketing it on a national level."
altText: A gray girl robot with orange hair, a red bow, and round glasses talks across two pastel panels, announcing she just heard the new Katy Perry song and delivering her verdict on it.
hoverText: You're gonna hear her roar. In establishments with a two-drink minimum.

title: The Reboot Singularity
transcript:
Panel 1. A blue robot at a computer says: "Huh... Reboot is rebooting." A red robot snaps: "That's it!"
Panel 2. The red robot rants: "Spider-Man! Superman! Star Trek! Fantastic Four! FULL HOUSE!!! We've hit the rebooting singularity!!!"
Panel 3. The red robot storms off in a scribble of rage while the blue robot raises a finger.
Panel 4. The blue robot says: "Fuller House is a continuation." The red robot screams: "SEMANTICS!!!"
altText: Four-panel comic. A red robot melts down about Spider-Man, Superman, Star Trek, Fantastic Four, and Full House all being rebooted, declaring the rebooting singularity, while a calm blue robot points out that Fuller House is technically a continuation. The red robot screams SEMANTICS!!!
hoverText: This site coming back after a decade offline is ALSO a continuation. SEMANTICS!!!

title: Chuck’s My Hero
transcript:
Panel 1. A gray robot holding a phone shouts: "CHUCK IS ON HEROES!!!"
Panel 2. A girl robot replies: "No, Zachary Levi is on Heroes Reborn." The gray robot: "Do you think Hiro can time travel back and help Chuck on old missions?"
Panel 3. The girl robot: "He's not Chuck on that show. Are you taking part in this conversation?"
Panel 4. The gray robot, dreamily: "Man... I love Chuck."
altText: Four-panel comic. A gray robot excitedly insists Chuck is on Heroes while a girl robot patiently explains it's actually Zachary Levi on Heroes Reborn. The gray robot concludes: Man... I love Chuck.
